page: Amazon Web Services i3.16xlarge is a storage-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, it features 64 virtual CPUs (32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled) running at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 488.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 7.62 GB per core. It operates on the Xen hypervisor and includes a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

A defining feature of this instance is its bundled local storage, consisting of 15,200 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. This massive local storage array directly supports high-throughput, low-latency I/O operations. In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong performance in Passmark database operations, placing in the top 25%. However, its single-core processing capabilities are relatively weak, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 10%. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and Redis SET operations consistently register in the average range.

From a resource density perspective, the i3.16xlarge offers a qualitative balance of high-capacity local NVMe storage and substantial memory, making it highly cost-efficient for workloads that require direct, fast access to large datasets without relying on external network-attached storage. The primary tradeoff is the older CPU architecture, which exhibits lower single-threaded efficiency and lacks AVX-512 instruction sets. Consequently, this instance is best suited for large-scale relational and non-relational databases, high-throughput transactional systems, data warehousing, and distributed file systems, rather than compute-heavy or single-threaded processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services i3.16xlarge is a storage-optimized instance designed for high-I/O workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 64 vCPUs (32 physical cores) and 488.0 GB of RAM, it features a massive 15,200 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage and a 25 Gbps baseline network speed. While single-core CPU benchmarks are weak, placing in the bottom 10%, the instance excels in database operations, scoring in the top 25% of Passmark database benchmarks. Multi-core and memory bandwidth performance remain average. This hardware profile offers high qualitative cost efficiency for storage-intensive applications by eliminating the need for external storage attachments. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, transactional systems, and distributed file systems.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i3.16xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 488 GB RAM, and 15,200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong database performance for high-I/O workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i3.16xlarge is a storage-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 488 GB RAM, and 15,200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A storage-optimized platform featuring massive local NVMe SSD capacity and substantial memory for high-throughput database and transactional workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 488.0 GB of system memory
15,200 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Strong database performance, ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
Best suited for large-scale databases and transactional systems
